targetServices.name: Commercial Appliance Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Highest-volume keyword cluster (8,100+/mo nationally); Armstrong currently not in top 100 for primary terms; competitors like Hobart Service and General Parts dominate above the fold

targetServices.name: Commercial Refrigeration Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Dedicated Houston and Austin geo terms with premium CPCs ($6.67–$13.56); Armstrong not in top 100 for any refrigeration terms; Zero Degree Refrigeration is closest local peer

targetServices.name: Commercial Fryer Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: High-urgency emergency dispatch service; service-specific landing page identified as Days 31–60 priority; no dedicated page currently exists on Armstrong site

targetServices.name: Walk-In Cooler Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Service-specific page identified as Days 31–60 build priority; critical for restaurant and hospitality segments; no dedicated page currently exists

targetServices.name: Commercial Ice Machine Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Explicitly called out in hotel/hospitality persona as high-fear failure scenario; service page identified as Days 31–60 priority; Manitowoc STAR authorization is key differentiator

targetServices.name: Commercial Oven Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Service-specific page identified as Days 31–60 build priority; critical for K-12 and institutional segments; no dedicated page currently exists

targetServices.name: Commercial Dishwasher Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Service-specific page identified as Days 31–60 build priority; relevant across all institutional segments; no dedicated page currently exists

targetServices.name: Commercial Kitchen Ventilation & Hood Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: 'Commercial kitchen hood repair' keyword at 320/mo nationally; Armstrong not in top 100; service page identified as Days 31–60 build priority

targetServices.name: Preventative Maintenance Contracts for Commercial Foodservice Equipment
targetServices.competitiveContext: Core contract vehicle for all six buyer segments; competitors win master service agreements through response-time SLAs and OEM authorization credentials; Armstrong's multi-location coverage is a key differentiator

targetServices.name: Multi-Location Master Service Agreements
targetServices.competitiveContext: Highest-LTV offering; national networks like General Parts and CKPS compete on consolidated billing and multi-site coverage; Armstrong's two-metro footprint positions it for Houston+Austin consolidation plays

targetServices.name: Emergency 24/7 Commercial Kitchen Equipment Dispatch
targetServices.competitiveContext: Stage 1 buyer motion; first credible local result with visible 24/7 capability wins the call; Armstrong's GBP and mobile presence gaps directly undermine capture of this demand

targetServices.name: K-12 School District Foodservice Equipment Repair & Scheduled Maintenance
targetServices.competitiveContext: Procurement-driven segment requiring district-approved vendor status, CFESA certification, and cooperative purchasing alignment (BuyBoard, TIPS-USA); Pasadena ISD and Crosby ISD are verified Armstrong references providing local competitive advantage national networks cannot replicate

targetServices.name: Healthcare & Senior Living Kitchen Equipment Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Compliance-first segment requiring CMS/Joint Commission documentation, vendor health-screening, and Texas Medical Center vendor list presence; longer sales cycle but high contract value and renewal stability

targetServices.name: Hotel & Hospitality Banquet Kitchen Equipment Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Brand-standard compliance and technician discretion are table stakes; hotel-brand approved-vendor list presence required for corporate properties; Houston Galleria/Medical Center and Austin downtown corridors are primary target zones

targetServices.name: Higher Education Dining Services Equipment Repair & Maintenance
targetServices.competitiveContext: Campus procurement pre-approval required; NACUFS network and cooperative purchasing programs (UT System, A&M System) are primary discovery channels; two-metro coverage is differentiator for multi-campus university systems

targetServices.name: Corporate Campus & Employee Café Equipment Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Austin tech corridor (Samsung, Tesla, Apple, Google) and Houston energy HQ segment; COI compliance, badge readiness, and predictable monthly billing are decision drivers; IFMA chapter presence is key discovery channel

targetServices.name: OEM-Authorized Commercial Foodservice Equipment Repair (Manitowoc, Hobart, Vulcan, Frymaster, Traulsen)
targetServices.competitiveContext: Factory authorization is table-stakes for procurement-driven segments; Manitowoc STAR, Blue Book, FER, Montague/Moffat/Belshaw credentials need schema markup to surface in AI platform recommendations; Hobart Service competes directly as manufacturer-owned service network

targetServices.name: Houston Commercial Foodservice Equipment Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: Geography page identified as Days 31–60 build priority; Armstrong ranks Pos 15 for 'commercial appliance repair houston' (90/mo) and not in top 100 for refrigeration terms; All Service Commercial Kitchen and CKPS are primary local competitors

targetServices.name: Austin Commercial Foodservice Equipment Repair
targetServices.competitiveContext: No Austin GBP exists — single highest-leverage local-search fix in the report; Armstrong ranks Pos 19–28 for limited Austin terms and not in top 100 for primary volume terms; creating Austin GBP is Day 1–30 priority
